text: The crystal structure of the recently discovered high-mobility semimetal Cd3As2 was reinvestigated by single-crystal X-ray diffraction. Contrary to previous studies reporting on noncentrosymmetric structure at room temperature and below, our data at 100 K shows that the Cd3As2 structure is centrosymmetric and thus in the I4 1/acd space group, with a = 12.633(3) Å and c = 25.427(7) Å. The structure can be considered as an ordered derivative of the antifluorite type, where each Cd6 □2 prism is distorted and arranged in columns with opposite chiralities along the c axis. First-principles electronic structure calculations using the centrosymmetric model predicted that the degeneracy of the Dirac node along the Γ-Z line at the Fermage level is not lifted, resulting in a four-fold degenerate Dirac point. Since all bands are preserved to be spin degenerate due to the presence of the inversion symmetry, Cd3As2 should still be considered as a 3D Dirac system, similar to that of graphene. Scanning tunneling microscopy experiments on the (112) cleaved surface of Cd3As2 reveals a c2 x 2 arrangement of the surface As atoms and we found that the preferred growth direction of needle-like single crystals is along the [110] direction.
